rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Bond is reduced to $107,000, based on the value of all of the personal property, not just cash. Keeping receipts is a Court-appointed fiduciary's duty, regardless of how cumbersome it is. No status hearing is being set for the new bond to be filed, showing that it covers the newly appointed successor co-trustee; the Court is relying on Counsel to make sure that bond for the two co-trustees is promptly filed. (Laticia Suazo's preferred mailing address is on Kirkland Street..?)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHEE WANG CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the "supplement" filed on 4/22/25 AMENDED (corrected and revised) the petition. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(a), and the issue of an LDA "assisting" the administrator was payment of fees without a Court order. A personal representative who pays fees from her personal funds and then seeks to be reimbursed for those fees is, essentially, paying fees without a prior Court order. (And it defies credibility that the administrator directed the LDA to file the papers she filed.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DORIS ANN NORDEEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) There was no tangible personal property in the estate for the Court to order any to be distributed. (3) The schedule of gains on sale shows different numbers of shares and carry values than inventoried, and there does not appear to be an informational schedule showing the changes. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] (4) The net amount appears to be incorrect if $1,816,442.71 was on hand as of 4/7/25. (5) The bank statement ends on 3/31/25, which precedes the ending date of the accounting period. The bank statement must encompass the ending date of the accounting period. (6) All of the cash, well in excess of the FDIC insured amount, is at one bank. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copies of the petition or any supporting papers were received by the examiner, which delayed review. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The will distributed fractional shares; it would be more accurate if the petition and order for final distribution showed fractions instead of percentages that do not total 100%.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDISON KWOCK FAI TSENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues must be cleared. 1. Re-Notice of Petition to Administer Estate to Pui Ching Middle School and Richard Wong Achuck. Notices filed 3/7/25 and 5/20/25 failed to meet statutory requirements because (1) Notice to Pui Ching Middle School was made 13 days, not 15 days, prior to 5/28/25 hearing. Prob. Code sec. 8110; (2) Notice of Directions for remote Appearances and Access has not been given, LRSF 14.1.C.6); (3) hearing time listed as 9:15, not 9:00 a.m.; and (4) #10 to list counsel's name, not the petitioners' names (5) caption set forth incorrect dept (301). 2. Attorney's declaration filed 4/8/2025 setting forth that petitioners were friends and caregivers of the decedent. Petitioner(s)'s verified declaration explaining further their relationship to the decedent further in relation to Prob. Code 21380 is required. --- relevant amended supplemental declaration filed 5/21/25 by the counsel without the petitioners' verifications 3. Any supplement needs to be served to Pui Ching Middle School and Richard Wong Achuck. Email service to Achuck must meet Prob. Code sec. 1215(c). 4. Petition #3.d. to be completed as to the estimated value of the property of the estate. (2)-(7) left as blank. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD WILLIAM LERNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Petitioner must sign and verify a supplement to her petition, addressing the following: When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Whose children are the niblings listed in item 8 of the petition? Please provide a courtesy copy of the supplement to the assigned examiner by 7/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A hearing might still be required to admit the will, if the two witnesses are the parents of sole beneficiary, who lives at the same address as the petitioner.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARLENE JOYCE DALY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Petitioner must sign and verify a supplement to her petition, addressing the following: (1) If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (Petitioner's supplement filed on 5/9/25 does not indicate whether there were any aunts, uncles, cousins, etc., the issue of the predeceased grandparents.) (2) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed yet. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
